content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 13, 2010

Dean C. Logan
Tim McNamara
Los Angeles County
Registrar-Recorder/County Clerk's Office
12400 Imperial Highway
Norwalk, CA 90650

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-10-005

Dear Sirs:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the Los Angeles County
Registrar-Recorder/County Clerk regarding the campaign filing officer duty provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your questions seek general guidance and do not
reference any specific campaign filing, we are treating your request as one for informal
assistance.²

QUESTIONS

You are requesting advice on whether a filing officer must request amendments to the
Form 460, campaign statement, in the following instances:

1. When a filer completes multiple pages of a particular Form 460 schedule and does not
include a separate subtotal for each continuation page of that schedule?

2. If a filer attaches a list of the filer's related committees instead of disclosing those
committees on Section 5 of the Form 460 Cover Page?

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory
references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices
Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All
regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)

File No. 1-10-005
Page No. 2

3. When a filer uses a post office box number in lieu of a street address, or if a filer only
reports "Payment Center" in lieu of a street address for a payee or creditor?

CONCLUSIONS

1. Regulation 18110 does not require a filing officer to request amendments if the subtotals
on continuation sheets are not provided.

2. In addition, Regulation 18110 does not require a filing officer to request an amendment if
information required in Section 5 of the Form 460 Cover page is clearly identified in an
attachment.

3. As the filing officer for campaign statements filed with the LA County, you are required
to determine whether campaign statements conform on their face with the requirements
of the Act, as specified in regulation 18110. Amendments must be requested for
statements that do not comply with the Act's disclosure requirements for identification of
a contribution or expenditure. However, you are not required to request an amendment
for errors or omissions that are minor and do not recur throughout a campaign statement.
See analysis for detailed information.

ANALYSIS

Duty to File

Candidates and committees are required to file campaign statements disclosing
contributions received and expenditures made (Section 84200 et seq.). The Act requires that
campaign statements for local committees be filed with the local filing officer (Section 84215).

Duties of the Filing Officer

As filing officer for Los Angeles County elections, campaign statements for candidates
and committees active in your jurisdiction are filed with your office. The Act requires that you
review those campaign statements to determine whether they conform on their face with the
requirements of the Act (Section 81010). When a campaign statement does not conform with the
requirements of the Act, you are required to request amendment(s).

Regulation 18110 sets out the rules for reviewing campaign statements. The regulation
provides a detailed list of information that campaign statements must disclose and if omitted the
filing officer should request an amendment. The regulation does not require that subtotals of
continuation sheets be recorded nor does it prohibit the use of an attachment for a filer to identify
related committees. Therefore, a filing officer should not request amendments seeking such
information.

File No. I-10-005
Page No. 3

Contributions and Addresses

Section 84211 requires committees to identify the street address of all contributions of
$100 or more (including monetary and nonmonetary contributions as well as loans received,
reported on Schedules A, B and C). The only exception for a street address is a contributor
serving on government duty outside the United States. (Regulation 18421.1.) Regulation
18110(b) (2) (E) requires a filing officer to determine if a contributor's complete address is
disclosed.

However, Regulation 18110(c)(3) provides that a filing officer does not need to request
an amendment when there are only minor errors and the errors do not recur throughout the
statement. As guidance, if a contributor's name and one of the following pieces of information
are provided, the omission is considered minor:¹

1. The contributor's address;
2. The contributor's occupation; or
3. The contributor's employer or principal place of business

Therefore, when a P.O. Box is disclosed in lieu of a street address, a filing officer is not
required to request an amendment as long as a contributor's name and occupation or the
contributor's name and employer are identified and the error does not occur throughout the
statement.

Expenditures and Addresses

Section 84211 also requires committees to identify the street address of all expenditures
of $100 or more (including payments made, accrued expenses and loans made, reported on
Schedules E, F, G and H) and Regulation 18110(b)(2)(E) requires a filing officer to determine if
the complete address of a recipient of an expenditure is disclosed. The use of "payment center"
or a P.O. Box for an expenditure address is not the same as a street address and should be
considered an incomplete address.

However, Regulation 18110(c) (3) provides that as long as the name of the person
providing consideration is identified, the omission of the street address and expenditure
description is considered a minor error and if the error does not recur throughout the statement,
the filing officer is not required to request an amendment.

Recurring Actions

A filing officer must use discretion in determining whether a minor error is so frequent
that an amendment request should be made. It is not possible in a regulation or an advice letter

¹ The Commission may be amending this regulation in the future.

File No. I-10-005
Page No. 4

to describe with particularity when an amendment is always triggered. However, a filing officer
should take into consideration the ratio of minor omissions to the number of entries.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

[signature]
By: Sandy Johnson
Political Reform Consultant

SJ:jgl

AI\ALYSN
Duty to File
Candidates and committees are required to file campaign statements disclosing
contributions received and expenditures made (Section 84200 et seq.). The Act requires that
campaign statements for local committees be filed with the local filing officer (Section 84215).
Duties of the Filing Officer
As filing officer for Los Angeles County elections, campaign statements for candidates
and committees active in your jurisdiction are filed with your office. The Act requires that you
review those campaign statements to determine whether they confonn on their face with the
requirements of the Act (Section 81010). When a campaign statement does not conform with the
requirements of the Act, you are required to request amendment(s).
Regulation 18110 sets out the rules for reviewing campaign statements. The regulation
provides a detailed list of information that campaign statements must disclose and if omitted the
filing officer should request an amendment. The regulation does not require that subtotals of
continuation sheets be recorded nor does it prohibit the use of an attachment for a filer to identify
related committees. Therefore, a filing officer should not request amendments seeking such
information.
C ontributions and Addresses
Section 84211requires committees to identify the street address of all contributions of
$100 or more (including monetary and nonmonetary contributions as well as loans received,
reported on Schedules A, B and C). The only exception for a street address is a contributor
serving on govemment duty outside the United States. (Regulation t8421.1.) Regulation
18110(b) (2) (E) requires a filing officer to determine if a contributor's complete address is
disclosed.
However, Regulation 18110(c)(3) provides that a filing officer does not need to request
an amendment when there are only minor errors and the errors do not recur throughout the
statement. As guidance, if a contributor's name and one of the following pieces of information
are provided, the omission is considered minor:3
l. The contributor's address;
2. The contributor's occupation; or
3. The contributor's employer or principal place of business
Therefore, when a P.O. Box is disclosed in lieu of a street address, a filing officer is not
required to request an amendment as long as a contributor's name and occupation or the
contributor's name and employer are identified and the error does not recur throughout the
statement.
Expenditures and Addresses
Section 84211also requires committees to identify the street address of all expenditures
of $100 or more (including payments made, accrued expenses and loans made, reportid on
Schedules E, F, G and H) and Regulation 18110(bX2)(E) requires a filing officer to determine if
the complete address of a recipient of an expenditure is disclosed. The use of "payment center"
or a P.O. Box for an expenditure address is not the same as a street address and should be
considered an incomplete address.
However, Regulation 18110(c) (3) provides that as long as the name of the person
providing consideration is identified, the omission of the street address and expenditure

the filing officer is not required to request an amendment.
Recurring Actions
A filing officer must use discretion in determining whether a minor error is so frequent
that an amendment request should be made. It is not possible in a regulation or an adviceletter
i The Commission may be amending this regulation in the future
to describe with particularity when an amendment is always triggered. However, a filing officer
should take into consideration the ratio of minor omissions to the number of entries.

(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) File No. 1-10-005 Page No. 2 3.
